videos.title: Cardiff UK - Complete City Travel Guide to the Welsh Capital - Wales City Tour
videos.description:
Welcome to Cardiff, the vibrant capital of Wales вАФ a city packed with history, culture, sport, and stunning natural beauty! If youвАЩve never been to Cardiff, nowвАЩs the time to add it to your travel bucket list.

In this video, IвАЩll take you on a full tour of everything you need to see and do in Cardiff, including:
рЯФє The medieval Cardiff Castle
рЯФє World-famous Principality Stadium (formerly Millennium Stadium)
рЯФє The National Museum of Wales
рЯФє Museum of Cardiff and the cityвАЩs rich local history
рЯФє Scenic Sophia Gardens & Bute Park
рЯФє The modern, waterside vibes of Cardiff Bay
вА¶ and so much more!

Whether you're into sports, history, architecture, or just looking for a great city break, Cardiff has something for everyone. From epic rugby matches to peaceful park strolls, itвАЩs a destination that blends tradition with modern Welsh culture perfectly.

рЯУН Perfect for weekend getaways, day trips, or exploring a different side of the UK.

рЯСЙ DonвАЩt forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more UK travel inspiration and city guides every week!

videos.transcript: Hi guys, welcome to another episode of Ultimate Bucket List. And on today's show; I show you around the Welsh capital city of Cardiff. Where to go, what to do, where to eat, and how to have an amazing time on a budget here in Wales. Welcome to the Welsh capital. It's located on the south coast of Wales. It's a city whereby Welsh culture mixes with modern amenities, and that's immediately evident all the way throughout the city. Now, if we have a look at a map of the city of Cardiff you'll notice that it's in two distinct parts: the main city of Cardiff, and Cardiff Bay, which is literally two miles away from the city of Cardiff. But let's focus right now on the actual city itself, which is very walkable and you don't need public transport to get around the place because everything is pretty much within walking distance. I suppose I better start by telling you about this big thing here. CardiffвАЩs most visited attraction and possibly its most visible. Cardiff Castle. From the street level it looks pretty grand and it is the main entrance is right on the front here technically you can walk around the grounds of the castle for free. Everything that you see in this video right now is technically free of charge. You literally just walk through the gates take as many photos and videos and walk around as much as you like. The best photo and video opportunities are around here so technically this could be free of charge. But if you do want to see the interior of the castle, that's when you'll need to buy a ticket. The ticket prices vary depending on what you want to do. In general, they allow you access to things like the Roman walls which is the original Roman wall that stood here. Nowadays, it's filled with wooden carvings which are quite intricate. It also allows you access to the keep, which is this big giant castle-like structure on the hill here. It looks very impressive from the outside, but from the inside - well there's actually not that much to it. In fact, it's pretty plain jane. I mean yes, you can actually walk up the stairs and explore some of the rooms in the keep, which there's nothing in it. But you're mainly going up there for the views. From the view at the top of the keep you can see the modern mixed with the ancient. And I think this video snapshot right here is indicative of the entire city of Cardiff. Once you finish with a keep, it's time to explore some of the interior bits of the castle, and once again they don't make it immediately obvious what your ticket entails. You're actually allowed to wander around some of the insides of the castle. You have the option of paying an extra ¬£4 or so to have a guide, guide you around the various areas of the castle. This is not mandatory you can actually access the bits of the castle without a guide, but they don't make that immediately obvious, so bear that in mind. I did pay for a guided tour - the best thing about these guided tours is that sometimes you'll learn a lot more about the places that you're seeing than if you were to go around the place on your own. So for example this is this winter smoking room. The tour guide does a good job in explaining all the symbolic things that you see on the walls and the ceilings, which you would probably never guess if you were just to have a look at this on your own time. You get to learn a lot about the Bute family, who effectively owned this castle and most of Cardiff. And the most impressive things when you're walking around the interior of Cardiff castle, is some of the ceiling detail. Some of the ceilings here are incredibly ornate and intricate. You would never guess that this was here in an old castle like this. Even if you go to a wide open space like this, the banqueting hall, have a look at the ceilings because every detail is meticulously designed. It really is an awe-inspiring place. They'll even show you around the living quarters and where the Bute family used to live day-to-day. But my personal favourite room is this. Believe it or not this is a rooftop garden, though if I had my way IвАЩd probably convert this to a giant hot tub. Cardiff castle is also home to various old World War II shelters around the perimeter of the castle. There are deep underground tunnels that you can walk and explore in, admittedly that won't take you very long. Be sure to walk around the Roman battlements right at the top of the castle and вА¶ ooh a duck! Be sure to have a walk around the moat as well, play giant chess if you want, to and explore the museum inside. But one of the most impressive things is actually the clock tower, and again inside it's pretty lavishly decorated. You get to see the clockwork, the bells, and the most impressive thing in Cardiff castle probably the summer smoking room. Which is right at the top of the bell tower. Boy it's amazing! I mean how do you even build this at the top of a clock tower? It's pretty ornately decorated and it makes for some good photo and video opportunities. So, once you're done with Cardiff castle, you'll probably notice a big giant uber modern structure literally across the way there. This is the Millennium Stadium aka the Principality, and it's currently the home to the Welsh national rugby union team, and it's the largest stadium in the country of Wales. If you can get rugby tickets I highly recommend that you attend a match, even if you don't know what rugby is. Or if there's no matches going on I recommend that you do a stadium tour here. Check out my other video to see what that's like. If you are a big fan of sports, Cardiff is home to some iconic things. You've got the Sport Wales National Centre, that encompasses a whole bunch of sports. The national hockey centre, the national tennis centre, and rather famously - this place. Formerly known as the SWALEC stadium, this is more famously known as Sophia Gardens. One of the country's most iconic cricket grounds, it's home to Glamorgan Cricket Club, but this is more well known as an international test venue. So you'll get international cricket matches being played here. But even if you don't like sports anyway come here because Sophia Gardens is in a massive park space, which I guarantee you'll enjoy walking around. Right across the river, is Bute Park. Yet another beautiful green space. Even if you come to Cardiff and just want to waste your day away in lovely green space, you can do that here at Bute Park. If you want to learn more about the city of Cardiff, I recommend that you walk into the centre of town where you'll come across the Museum of Cardiff. It's free to go inside, and you can learn all about the history and the future of the city of Cardiff. Here it's got some interesting artefacts but admittedly I didn't stick around all that much. If museums is your thing, the national museum of Wales is located nearby. Also, and once again it's kind of an art/history gallery and you can definitely waste away an afternoon here. But when you're walking around the city centre you'll notice plenty of places that will sell you Welsh things most notably the Welsh love spoon. What is a Welsh love spoon? Well it's not that because that's a stuffed sheep, and those are stuffed dragons. You can buy typical tourist stuff here in these shops, but the wooden Welsh love spoon is kind of a historic superstitious item that you would give to your loved one. And here in this shop is the world's largest love spoon carved out of a single piece of wood. Wow that's a really big love spoon! You'll notice some historic places like the historic Cardiff market, and the Cardiff high street. All the shopping arcades seem to be located in these historic buildings, and it's a cool place to explore and check out some of the architecture, and some of the more quirky shops that you'll find here in Wales. But if historic doesn't appeal to you, they've also got modern shopping centres such as this the Saint DavidвАЩs Centre which is located right in the middle of town. You also won't struggle to find places that will serve you food and drink particularly in these traditional looking pubs. If you're looking for something typically Welsh to eat and drink, I recommend that you try the f√°gg√≥ts. IвАЩm not joking they're actually called that. And wash it down with a pint of Brains beer, which is brewed right here in the city of Cardiff. You can even check out the brewery quarter if you fancy a pint. But once you've had your fill of Brains and f√°gg√≥ts, it's now time to go towards the other part of Cardiff. Separating Cardiff Bay and the city of Cardiff, is this one and a half mile stretch of literally nothing. So yeah there's nothing to report here in this long stretch of road, but when you do get to Cardiff Bay it's kind of like a harbour area for the city of Cardiff, because well you're in a bay, and it has some very interesting things to see such as this the Wales Millennium Centre. Contrary to its title it's a big giant theatre where you can watch plays, and musicals, and shows. You can even watch the philharmonic orchestra here. This is Mermaid Quay. Usually it's a happening spot full of performances and live shows. On the day that I went it was pretty crappy weather and nothing was going on, so yeah, probably not the best example of that. This is the Welsh Parliament Building and if you wanted to, you could attend Parliament. But trust me it's totally not worth it. The orange building that you're seeing in front of you is the famous Pierhead Building. Originally it was the exhibition and trade centre, but is now mainly an exhibition which just happened to be closed when I got there. Have a walk around Cardiff Bay anyway because it's actually quite a nice place to have a wander around and when you do wander around Cardiff Bay. You'll probably notice quite a few weird things like this the Norwegian church. Why is there a Norwegian church in the middle of Cardiff? IвАЩm not too sure? But when you go inside, I don't know if this is typical of Norway IвАЩve never actually been. But when you go inside you can also wander around the cafe and art section, and buy yourself lots of Norwegian themed things, even though you're not in Norway. It also has a selection of bars and eateries like you would find anywhere else, but given that the weather turned really really bad whilst I was here, I decided to get the hell out of here without exploring too much more. IвАЩve been to Cardiff Bay before and it's infinitely nicer with friends, not when you're on your own like me. Now regulars to my channel will know that I tend to do quite a bit of stadium tours. No doubt some of you will be asking me if I can do a football tour here. Well, here at the former Ninian Park is now Cardiff City Stadium, the home ground for Cardiff City football club and the Welsh national football team. However вА¶ вАЬand before you ask, no Cardiff City currently don't do football tours. Especially not seven o'clock on a Sunday morningвАЭ Honestly I probably wouldn't have done a tour here anyway, but yeah apparently you can do tours here, There are two more castles that I highly recommend that you visit. The problem is they're nowhere near Cardiff. If you're prepared to drive half an hour north you'll get to the town of Caerphilly, where you'll find this slap bang in the middle of town. This is Caerphilly castle and it's one of the most interesting ruined castles that you'll come across here in the country of Wales. I think it's also the biggest but don't quote me on that. But it's definitely worth driving 30 minutes north just to see this place. And five minutes away from that is the infamous Castell Coch, which means вАШRed CastleвАЩ in Welsh. The Red Castle looks like a very medieval fairy tale castle and the reason why it's called the Red Castle is because well most of it's decked out in red. It's actually quite a modern castle by today's standards, and it's very interesting to visit inside. It's located in the middle of nowhere and if you do drive to the Red Castle, honestly be very careful, because the roads are mainly single track, and there's lots of blind corners everywhere. So please, be careful when driving to Caerphilly castle and the Red Castle. Overall guys, Cardiff is a place that you've got to visit once in your life. Especially if you're anywhere near Bath or Bristol, or if you're catching a concert or a rugby game. It's definitely worth exploring and immersing yourself in Welsh culture. Okay Ninh, IвАЩm sold. what do I need to do? Well you need to come here to the city of Cardiff. It's easily accessible by road via the M4 and M5, and it also has its own international airport; Cardiff international. The main train station Cardiff Central, and Cardiff Bay also has its own train station. You can also reach Cardiff via public transport. The cost? Well, Cardiff is actually quite a reasonably priced city - both in terms of food drink and attractions. Some of the attractions mentioned in this video are actually free. The only really expensive thing is if you get tickets to the rugby, if you want a decent seat you'll be paying through your nose for it. If you're looking for a place to stay Cardiff has a plethora of hotels that you can stay at, at reasonable prices. But given that it's slap bang in the middle of a city centre, parking is at a premium. When I stayed here I stayed about a 15 minute drive outside the city of Cardiff at cheap hotels that cost you about ¬£30 a night, so fairly reasonably priced, if you're prepared to stay outside of Cardiff. Is there anything else I need to know? Yes, the official language here is Welsh. And even though the signs are in both languages, most people speak English. So if you're a fluent English speaker you'll be absolutely fine here. The city itself is very walkable so you won't really need public transportation to get you to the various attractions mentioned in this video. If you've got a hard time walking you could catch a train between Cardiff Central and Cardiff Bay stations, but to be honest it's quite an easy walk. The weather here in Cardiff is incredibly unpredictable, so you've probably noticed in this video that when I first started filming it was sunny, and when I ended up in Cardiff Bay it was rainy. So bear that in mind the weather could turn at a drop of a hat. And finally because Wales is technically a separate country to England they also have different laws. There's not too many that will affect you day to day, but it's always advisable to check out the website just to see if the laws differ from that of England. So guys if you have enjoyed this video, please be sure to like share and subscribe. Comment on the comment section below, and if you've got any other bucket list ideas, you know what to do. If I get enough suggestions, IвАЩll go ahead and do that. So guys, thank you very much for watching and we'll see you in the next episode. Ninh Ly вАУ www.ninh.co.uk - @NinhLyUK

videos.title: I Explored The 5 Most DANGEROUS Areas in CARDIFF (w/Stats)
videos.description:
In this video, we take a deep dive into the most dangerous areas and estates in Cardiff.

Cardiff is a fantastic city with a large student population and some awesome modern amenities, but it also has some neighborhoods with alarmingly high crime rates. 

Join me as I explore these areas and provide official statistics to give you a comprehensive look at the situation.

I visited Caerau, Riverside & Pontcanna, Adamsdown, Butetown, and Cathays, uncovering the realities of these neighborhoods.

From street-level insights to detailed crime data, this video will give you a clearer picture of Cardiff's darker side.

Whether you're a local resident, planning to move to Cardiff, or just curious about the city's underbelly, this video is for you.

I hope you enjoyed the video, if you have any suggestions for other cities I should explore in a similar fashion please leave a comment below, thanks!

#cardiff #wales  #cardiffcity

videos.title: TOP 10 Things to do in Cardiff, Wales 2023!
videos.description:
TOP 10 Things to do in Cardiff, Wales 2023! 

Cardiff, Wales is an amazing place to visit in Wales. If you want to know top 10 what to do in Cardiff or you need a travel guide, please keep watching

4. Cardiff Bay Barrage: 
The Cardiff Bay Barrage is a sea defense located in Cardiff Bay. It stretches for 1.1 kilometers, providing a scenic promenade for pedestrians and cyclists. The barrage offers stunning views of the bay, with its shimmering waters and iconic landmarks. Along the way, visitors can stop at various viewpoints to take in the picturesque surroundings, making it a popular spot for leisurely walks and enjoying the outdoors.

3. Cardiff City Centre:
Cardiff City Centre is a vibrant and bustling hub in the Welsh capital. It offers a diverse range of attractions, from high-street shops and designer boutiques to charming arcades filled with independent stores. The city center is also known for its lively entertainment scene, with numerous theaters, cinemas, and music venues. It's a dynamic and lively place where visitors can immerse themselves in the energy of the city.

2. Bute Park: 
Bute Park is a serene oasis located in the heart of Cardiff, adjacent to Cardiff Castle. Spanning over 130 acres, it offers a peaceful retreat with its lush green spaces, meandering rivers, and picturesque scenery. Visitors can enjoy leisurely walks or bike rides along the park's numerous paths, relax in tranquil gardens, and take in stunning views of the historic castle and surrounding landmarks.

1. Cardiff Bay Wetlands Reserve: 
The Cardiff Bay Wetlands Reserve is a serene natural sanctuary nestled within the bustling Cardiff Bay area. This urban wetland provides a haven for diverse bird species and other wildlife, making it a paradise for nature enthusiasts. Visitors can explore the reserve's walking trails, bird hides, and observation points, allowing them to observe and appreciate the beauty of the wetland ecosystem right in the heart of the city.

videos.transcript: welcome to the travel boss in this video we're going to explore the top 10 things to do in Cardiff Cardiff the capital city of Wales is a vibrant and Cosmopolitan destination that seamlessly Blends Rich history with modern charm Nestle loved the banks of the river Taft Cardiff offers a captivating mix of cultural attractions stunning architecture and a lively atmosphere the city is renowned for its iconic landmarks such as Cardiff Castle and the principality Stadium which reflect its fascinating past and passionate sporting culture so let's get started 10. Cardiff Castle the Cardiff Castle is a magnificent historical landmark located in the heart of Cardiff with its Origins dating back to Roman times the castle has witnessed centuries of fascinating history its architecture combines Roman Norman and Victorian influences offering a captivating blend of styles visitors can explore its intricately decorated Interiors climb the imposing keep and wander through beautifully landscaped Gardens experiencing the rich Heritage of this iconic site 9. Cardiff Bay Cardiff Bay is a vibrant Waterfront area in the heart of Cardiff formerly a bustling dock it is transformed into a lively Entertainment District with a picturesque Marina the bay is home to iconic landmarks like the Wales Millennium Center and offers a plethora of dining options shops and cultural attractions visitors can enjoy scenic walks along the Waterfront or take boat trips to explore the Bay's Beauty from a different perspective 8. National Museum Cardiff the national museum Cardiff is a cultural gem located in the Heart of the City it houses an impressive collection of art archeology and natural history showcasing the diverse Heritage of Wales visitors can admire works by renowned artists delve into the country's ancient past and explore interactive displays that bring the exhibits to life the museum offers a fascinating glimpse into the rich tapestry of Welsh culture and history 7. principality Stadium this stadium is a renowned sporting and entertainment venue with a seating capacity of over 74 thousand it is famous for hosting International Rugby matches including Wales home games the Stadium's retractable roof allows for events to take place in all weather conditions adding to its versatility its iconic design and electric atmosphere make it a must visit destination for sports enthusiasts and Spectators alike 6. Cardiff Market Cardiff Market located in the heart of the city is a bustling and vibrant Hub that offers a diverse range of goods and experiences with its Charming Victorian architecture the market is home to a wide array of stalls selling fresh produce artisanal crafts clothing and traditional Welsh products it's a must visit destination to immerse yourself in the local atmosphere and Sample the flavors of karda 4. Cardiff Bay Burridge the Cardiff Bay barrage is a sea defense located in Cardiff Bay it stretches for 1.1 kilometers providing a Scenic Promenade for pedestrians and cyclists The Barrage offers stunning views of the bay with its shimmering Waters and iconic landmarks along the way visitors can stop at various viewpoints to take in the picturesque surroundings making it a popular spot for leisurely walks and enjoying the outdoors 3. Cardiff City Center Cardiff City Center is a vibrant and bustling Hub in the Welsh Capital it offers a diverse range of attractions from High Street shops and designer boutiques to Charming arcades filled with independent stores the city center is also known for its Lively entertainment scene with numerous theaters Cinemas and music venues it's a dynamic and lively place where visitors can immerse themselves in the energy of the city 2. Duke park Duke Park is a Serene Oasis located in the heart of Cardiff adjacent to Cardiff Castle spanning over 130 Acres it offers a peaceful retreat with its lush green spaces Meandering rivers and picturesque scenery visitors can enjoy leisurely walks or bike rides along the Park's numerous paths relax in tranquil Gardens and take in stunning views of the historic castle and surrounding landmarks 1. Cardiff Bay Wetlands Reserve the Cardiff Bay Wetlands Reserve is a Serene natural Sanctuary nestled within the bustling Cardiff Bay Area this Urban Wetland provides a Haven for diverse bird species and other Wildlife making it a paradise for nature enthusiasts visitors can explore the reserve's walking trails bird hides and observation points allowing them to observe and appreciate the beauty of the Wetland ecosystem right in the Heart of the City that's all for today if you want to see more videos like this make sure to hit that subscribe button and turn on notifications so that you'd never miss an upload
